findings.summary: Planned difficulty scaling feature (Phase 3) that model review correctly identified as wrong abstraction — dropped after review
findings.evidence: Plan v1 had ~50 lines of difficulty scaling; both GPT-5.4 and Gemini 3.1 Pro said bottleneck is eval noise not difficulty
findings.proposed_fix: Run baseline eval BEFORE planning autoresearch experiments. Probe-before-build rule 7 applies to experiment design.

findings.summary: Assumed 12+5 proposal-ranker test cases were adequate. Baseline was 100% — cases already solved by hardcoded priorities.
findings.evidence: python3 eval.py -> accuracy: 1.000000 on both dev and holdout splits
findings.proposed_fix: Add preflight baseline check to autoresearch.py — warn if baseline >= 95%

findings.summary: Dispatched 55K-token commit subagent that got permission-blocked on .claude/rules/ file. Committed manually anyway.
findings.evidence: Agent a2fc56d0 blocked on Edit/Write to .claude/rules/research-index.md
findings.proposed_fix: Don't dispatch subagents for commits — they're not fan-out work and rules files get permission-gated

findings.summary: User asked evaluative question, got multi-phase implementation plan. User corrected with 'not do stuff that's overengineered'.
findings.evidence: User: 'can we learn anything from this for us?' -> 4 research memos + cross-model review + 4-phase plan
findings.proposed_fix: Evaluative questions get assessment + optional next steps, not implementation plans
